channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: 28 agencies were authorized to fly helicopters near Reagan Airport before deadly crash
item.description: The Army was one of 28 government agencies authorized to fly helicopters near Ronald Reagan National Airport before its Black Hawk helicopter collided with an American Airlines passenger jet last week, killing 67.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: CDC staff told to remove terms like 'non-binary,' 'they/them,' 'pregnant people' from public health material
item.description: Researchers at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention have been told to remove words frequently associated with progressive gender ideology from research manuscripts that they intend to publish.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk team’s access to student loan systems raises alarm over borrowers' personal information
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democrats are pushing back against Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency as it turns its attention to the Education Department, with lawmakers raising concerns about DOGE’s access to internal systems containing personal information on tens of millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justice Department says it won't release the names of FBI agents who worked on Jan. 6 riot cases
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department has agreed to refrain from publicly identifying any FBI agents whose conduct is under review as President Donald Trump’s administration examines the investigations into the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sotomayor criticizes presidential immunity case as putting the high court’s legitimacy on the line
item.description: In her first public comments since the start of Trump's second term, Justice Sotomayor said she believes the ruling puts the Court's legitimacy at risk.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kash Patel had a roster of foreign clients. Their interests could clash with FBI he hopes to lead
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Kash Patel, President Donald Trump’s pick to lead the FBI, holds more than $1 million of stock in a fashion company founded in China. He established a nonprofit that spent big on promotion but little on its mission.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN SLODYSKO, ERIC TUCKER and ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration withdraws support for transgender minors in Tennessee case at the Supreme Court
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal government no longer backs transgender minors and their families in Tennessee who are challenging a state ban on gender-affirming care, the Trump administration told the Supreme Court Friday.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats ask for an investigation into DOGE's access to Treasury's payment systems
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democratic lawmakers are seeking a Treasury Department investigation of the access that Elon Musk's team was given to the government's payment system, citing “threats to the economy and national security, and the potential violation of laws protecting Americans’ privacy and tax dat
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More active duty troops will head to US-Mexico border, bringing the total to 3,600
item.description: The Pentagon will deploy roughly 1,500 more active duty soldiers to the southern border to support President Donald Trump’s expanding crackdown on immigration.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump to sign executive order establishing White House faith office
item.description: President Donald Trump is expected to sign an executive order establishing a White House Faith Office on Friday, after promising to end the "War on Christians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump plans to meet with Zelenskyy as he looks to end Ukraine war
item.description: President Donald Trump said that Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy may meet with him in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fentanyl's financial grip on US skyrocketed to $2.7T at height of Biden admin: study
item.description: The opioid crisis in 2023, amid the Biden administration, cost the U.S. $2.7 trillion, according to a Council of Economic Advisers study exclusively obtained by Fox Digital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats try to enter Department of Education amid outrage over possible DOGE cuts
item.description: Democrat lawmakers attempted to enter the Department of Education building in Washington, but were blocked by a security guard who did not detail why he would not let them enter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: The Pentagon will deploy roughly 1,500 more troops to the southern border
item.description: The Pentagon will deploy roughly 1,500 more active duty soldiers to the southern border to support President Trump’s expanding crackdown on immigration, a U.S. official said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Member of Elon Musk’s DOGE team resigns after racist posts resurface
item.description: Marko Elez had recently been given special access by a federal judge to highly sensitive payment systems in the Department of Treasury.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn and Shannon Bond, MPR News

item.title: Trump doubles down on Gaza Strip idea, says no US soldiers needed
item.description: President Trump on Thursday doubled down on his proposal for the U.S. to take control of the Gaza Strip and rebuild it.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Ben Stiller denies being paid by USAID to go to Ukraine
item.description: The reportedly debunked social media post was shared by billionaire Elon Musk, who owns X, and Donald Trump Jr..
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: 60K federal workers ready to take buyouts as judge blocks effort
item.description: So far, more than 60,000 employees, about 2% of the federal civilian workforce, have accepted the buyout.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Why CBS stands at the epicenter of Trump's assault on the media
item.description: CBS News appears likely to settle a lawsuit brought by President Trump in a case that legal observers say it should win. The network is caught up in Trump's broader assault on the media and an owner intent on finalizing a sale.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota Supreme Court hears arguments on House conflict despite GOP, DFL agreement
item.description: The Minnesota Supreme Court heard oral arguments Thursday morning in a case involving a standoff in the Minnesota House that has dragged on for more than three weeks, though GOP and DFL leaders had reached an agreement Wednesday night to resolve the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Renters' rights and 13 more bills to watch at the 2025 Nevada Legislature
item.description: Lawmakers are busy in Carson City drawing up new rules to live by, including tougher penalties for committing crimes, protections for transgender Nevadans and a variety of laws related to housing and renters' rights.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada proposal would add selling deadly fentanyl to first-degree murder charge
item.description: A proposal at the Nevada Legislature would amend a charge of first-degree murder to include providing fentanyl to a person who dies from an overdose.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Walz sets special election for seat in suburban Ramsey County that will decide House power
item.description: A special election will fill a seat won by a Democrat in the November election that came open after a residency challenge. The result could return the Minnesota House to a tie or boost a GOP majority.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs executive order intended to bar transgender athletes from girls‘ and women‘s sports
item.description: President Donald Trump has signed an executive order designed to prevent transgender athletes from participating in girls’ or women’s sports.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Dozens of Education Department staffers on leave amid Trump's DEI purge
item.description: Dozens of employees with the Department of Education were placed on administrative leave on Friday, allegedly after attending a diversity training course encouraged by then-Secretary Betsy DeVos during the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: DeSantis threatens Veto amid heated showdown
item.description: State lawmakers voted to pass the TRUMP act, which aims to support the president on immigration enforcement. Now, all eyes are on Governor DeSantis who promises a veto.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
